stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 4, 2026: consolidated PAT +30% YoY to Rs 1,124 cr (FY26 PAT +21% to Rs 3,797 cr); revenue +12% YoY to Rs 15,877 cr. NBFC AUM +27% YoY to Rs 1,59,916 cr; housing finance AUM +53% YoY to Rs 47,452 cr; lending portfolio crossed Rs 2 lakh cr. ROA improved 63 bps YoY to 2.07%. Life insurance VNB margin expanded 260 bps YoY to 20.6%; health insurance combined ratio improved from 105% (FY25) to 103% (FY26). Board approved increased borrowing limit to Rs 2 lakh cr. Rs 2,750 cr equity infusion from Advent International into Aditya Birla Housing Finance concluded April 2026. Motilal Oswal reiterated Buy with TP Rs 325 (raised from Rs 250); Morgan Stanley Overweight with TP Rs 320. CMP ~Rs 340-362; market cap ~Rs 94,995 cr.
stocks.sector_context: NBFC sector tailwind: RBI delivered 125 bps cumulative repo cuts through FY25 with easing bias into FY26; sector AUM expected to grow 15-19% to ~Rs 48-50 lakh cr by Mar 2026. Housing finance/LAP segments favored as EMIs fall (home loan AUM +12-13%, LAP +20%+). Bank credit growth printed at 15.9% in FY26, with NBFCs as the faster-growing segment. RBI NBFC amendment effective July 1, 2026 favors large diversified players. Microfinance and unsecured personal lending remain the weak pockets, with sector RoMA expected to compress 30-50 bps vs FY24.
stocks.peer_comparison: Significant outperformance vs peers on 1Y basis: ABCAPITAL +75-98% vs Bajaj Finance -5% and Cholamandalam +10%. Trades at TTM P/E ~25.2-25.7x, ~22% premium to industry average of 21x but at a discount to Bajaj Finance (~31x). PB ~2.9x. Health insurance GWP +39% YoY and life insurance individual FYP +15% YoY in FY26 - segment growth ahead of most peers. Diversified model (lending + AMC + life/health insurance) provides resilience vs mono-line NBFCs facing unsecured lending stress.
red_flags: Margin pressure flagged by analysts despite headline profit surge (Q4 commentary)
Valuation premium ~22% above industry average after sharp 1Y rally raises mean-reversion risk
Stock has run hard into the print, limiting near-term upside surprise potential
No specific ASM/GSM or SEBI action identified; promoter (Grasim 52.54%) shareholding unencumbered
catalysts: Post Q4 results conference call commentary on FY27 guidance
Borrowing limit hike to Rs 2 lakh cr enables continued AUM growth
Rs 2,750 cr Advent infusion in housing finance subsidiary supports AUM acceleration
Potential further RBI rate cuts in FY26 supportive over time
Possible value-unlock/listing optionality in subsidiaries (insurance, AMC) over medium term

stocks.recent_news: Stock rallied >3% intraday on May 5, 2026 to high of Rs 7,010; lifetime high Rs 7,200 on April 30, 2026; +51% since end-September 2025. Q4 FY26 print (declared ~April 29-30, 2026) materially beat estimates: revenue +33.8% YoY to Rs 938 cr (est Rs 807 cr); EBITDA Rs 322 cr +80% YoY (est Rs 254 cr) with margin 34.3% (+875 bps); PAT +114% YoY to Rs 213 cr (est Rs 156 cr). FY26 full-year: revenue Rs 3,314 cr (+41%), operating EBITDA Rs 1,082 cr (+103%, margin 32.6%), PAT Rs 664 cr vs Rs 289 cr. Final dividend Rs 8.6/share (430% of Rs 2 face value). Six consecutive quarters of revenue and profit growth driven by Specialty Chemicals, CDMO, and HPP verticals. Geojit issued bullish call May 5 with 3-6 month target Rs 8,500 (~21% upside).
stocks.sector_context: Indian specialty chemicals sector saw a tepid H1 FY26 amid US tariffs (R32/R125, MMA, 2,4-D affected) and weak agrochem demand from excess rainfall, but FY27 narrative improving as capacity expansions ramp at SRF, NFIL, Gujarat Fluorochemicals. China+1 tailwind intact; India specialty chemicals exports doubled $12 bn (2020) to $24 bn (2025); domestic market growing toward USD 90+ bn by 2030. Fluorochemicals sub-segment has structural demand pull from refrigerants (R-32), agrochem and CDMO. Iran-related geopolitical volatility earlier in 2026 lifted fluorine prices.
stocks.peer_comparison: Navin's Q4 PAT growth of +114% YoY and EBITDA margin 34.3% lead the listed fluorine basket vs Aarti Industries (Q4 PAT +26.6% YoY), SRF, Gujarat Fluorochemicals and Deepak Nitrite. FY27 expected revenue growth ~28% well ahead of Aarti (~11%). EBITDA margin sits at the high end of the specialty chemicals peer set, reflecting CDMO and HPP mix. NFIL is among the strongest 6-month performers in the basket on Chemours, AHF and CDMO ramp story.
red_flags: Stock at lifetime-high zone (Rs 7,200 on Apr 30) - already ~80% of Geojit Rs 8,500 target after 51% rally in 7 months; risk-reward stretched short term
Some analysts flagging Q4 margin sustainability question - 34.3% EBITDA margin includes favorable mix that may normalize
Forward growth concentrated in a few CDMO contracts, Chemours project execution, and HFC/AHF capacity ramp
US tariff overhang on certain HFC/refrigerant exports remains a sector-level headwind
Refrigerant pricing cyclicality could squeeze margins if global demand softens
catalysts: Chemours Opteon 2P50 two-phase immersion cooling fluid project at Surat on track for commissioning end-June/early-July 2026 (1QFY27); USD 14 mn capex with Chemours contributing USD 5 mn
AHF (anhydrous HF) capacity commissioning supporting ~28% FY27 revenue growth guide
Additional HFC capacity ramp expected in FY27; CDMO order pipeline conversion
Final dividend Rs 8.6/share - record date and AGM filings to be tracked
All promoter pledge released March 20, 2026 (Mafatlal Impex 25.44% now fully unencumbered) - clean shareholding post-overhang removal
Geojit Rs 8,500 technical target continues to support institutional momentum
